title: Knautia lancifolia
intro: Knautia lancifolia, also known as cut-leaved bluet, is a perennial plant belonging to the Caprifoliaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues and as an antiseptic to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Its bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Knautia lancifolia include teas and infusions, as well as tinctures and extracts used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Knautia lancifolia is a member of the plant family Caprifoliaceae. This family is commonly known as the honeysuckle or caprifoliaceous family. The Caprifoliaceae family is part of the larger order Dipsacales. Dipsacales is a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Adoxaceae and Valerianaceae. The classification of Knautia lancifolia reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plant species within the same order.
